mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with FEDFUNDS changes (Rank IC < 0.02) across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.5%, labor market deterioration signals impending earnings declines and recession risk, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or the signal loses predictive power (t-stat < 1.5) in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and industrial production confirms broad-based demand growth, which directly boosts corporate revenues and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform individual series, with interaction term coefficients statistically indistinguishable from zero in OLS regressions over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of persistent inflation above 3%, rising CPIAUCSL values force markets to price in aggressive monetary tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL increases fail to predict negative 5-day returns when inflation exceeds 3%, with out-of-sample R-squared dropping below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating housing starts indicate rising consumer confidence and construction sector activity, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Housing starts growth shows zero correlation with 60-day equity returns, and the signal generates negative alpha in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal supply-side contraction and weakening manufacturing demand, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Consecutive INDPRO declines do not precede negative market moves, with win rates below 40% in triggered samples.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion, supporting consumer spending and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal loses predictive accuracy when PAYEMS and UNRATE move in opposite directions, yielding Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding 2% annualized confirms macroeconomic stability, reducing recession probabilities and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: GDPC1 readings above 2% fail to predict positive 5-day returns, with the signal showing negative information coefficients in walk-forward validation.
